title: Test one execution model for production actions and coordinated cues

claims.statement: Discovery established that one physical control may need to execute either one production action or a coordinated workflow.
claims.evidence: Doctrine-Driven Development Discovery for the Linux operator capability

claims.statement: ADR-0005 demonstrates only one explicitly targeted and idempotent component visibility intent inside the in-process production domain.
Rollback of an already visible live-production effect is not demonstrated and cannot be assumed safe.
claims.evidence: ADR-0005#receipt-scope-is-honest and the absence of compensation evidence

claims.statement: H-002: An immutable Show-scoped cue with one explicit target and ordered typed intents can represent both one action and a coordinated workflow when execution is fail-fast, replay is idempotent, and the aggregate receipt makes every completed or failed step explicit.

Review accepted H-002 only for in-process sequential composition after one-step, multi-step, partial-failure, replay, conflict, authorization, and isolation tests passed; atomic rollback and external observation were not inferred.
This Slice does not determine waits, schedules, parallel branches, compensation, durable recovery, network transport, hardware state images, or device identity.

successCriteria.statement: A canonical cue names its Show, target bus, stable identifier, and one to twenty ordered typed steps; one action is represented as a one-step cue.
successCriteria.verification: Protocol type checks and cue validation tests.

successCriteria.statement: Cue execution requires an expected initial target revision and one operation identifier, derives deterministic child operation identifiers, and preserves ADR-0005 Program authorization.
A successful cue returns one completed aggregate receipt containing ordered per-step receipts and the final target revision.
A cue stops at the first failed step and returns one failed aggregate receipt that identifies completed steps, the failed step, its error, and the resulting target revision without claiming rollback.
Replaying the same cue operation returns the original aggregate receipt without applying steps twice, while reusing the operation identifier for another cue conflicts.
Cue execution changes only the named runtime bus and never mutates the saved Scene supplied to Production.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0011 records SDD-002 as an experiment and separates established ADR-0005 knowledge from H-002 and remaining unknowns.
Only cue contracts, in-process composition, and focused tests are added; no transport, UI, credential, or hardware claim enters the Slice.
Review evaluates whether H-002 unified one and many intents while making partial execution unambiguous rather than pretending it was atomic.
ADR-0006 was created only after the Slice evidence was reviewed and the human authority accepted H-002 within the demonstrated sequential in-process boundary.
Governance, quality, focused tests, and build suites pass for the final Slice manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check and npm run check and npm run build
